item.title: Search ends in relief as boat carrying five people is found
item.description: Five people, including a child, have been rescued after the boat they were travelling on between Korolevu and Vatulele became overdue. The Republic of Fiji Navy says the Joint Rescue Coordination Centre Fiji was activated last night after reports were received that the boat failed to arrive at the expected time.
item.content:encoded:
Five people, including a child, have been rescued after the boat they were travelling on between Korolevu and Vatulele became overdue.

The Republic of Fiji Navy says the Joint Rescue Coordination Centre Fiji was activated last night after reports were received that the boat failed to arrive at the expected time.

RFNS Savenaca was deployed in the early hours of this morning. With assistance from a New Zealand Defence Force P-8 aircraft and two domestic aircraft carrying Navy search and rescue personnel, the boat was successfully located in rough seas.

The New Zealand Defence Force P-8 aircraft was also on its way to assist with another search and rescue operation involving an overdue boat in Kiribati, which is being coordinated by JRCC Fiji.

The Republic of Fiji Navy has thanked everyone involved in the operation and is urging all mariners, especially small boat operators, to avoid unnecessary travel during bad weather conditions.

item.title: Direct Gold Coast flight opens new opportunities for Fiji
item.description: For thousands of Fijian families with loved ones living in Queensland, travelling between Fiji and Australia has just become easier. The launch of Fiji Airways' inaugural direct flight between Nadi and the Gold Coast today marks a significant milestone not only for the national carrier but also for Fiji's growing economic and people-to-people ties with Australia.
item.content:encoded:
For thousands of Fijian families with loved ones living in Queensland, travelling between Fiji and Australia has just become easier.

The launch of Fiji Airways' inaugural direct flight between Nadi and the Gold Coast today marks a significant milestone not only for the national carrier but also for Fiji's growing economic and people-to-people ties with Australia.

Speaking at the launch, Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka described the new route as far more than the launch of a new air route, saying it opens a new gateway of opportunity between Fiji and one of Australia's fastest-growing regions.

Rabuka says the new service will provide more than 53,000 seats annually between Fiji and the Gold Coast, creating easier access for tourists, business travellers, students and families travelling between the two destinations.

He says the direct connection will strengthen the renewed Vuvale Partnership between Fiji and Australia while supporting Fiji's long-term development goals under Vision 2050.

He says it is a gateway that strengthens our people-to-people ties, expand economic engagement and reflects the deepening relationship between Fiji and Australia.

The Prime Minister also highlighted the Gold Coast's status as one of Australia's most vibrant urban economies, known for its tourism sector, sporting culture and rapidly expanding population.

He says the direct route will eliminate the need for many travellers to transit through other Australian cities, making journeys simpler, faster and more convenient.

He adds that tourism remains a cornerstone of Fiji's economy, supporting thousands of jobs across hotels, resorts, transport, agriculture, retail businesses and community enterprises.

He says Australia continues to be Fiji's largest tourism market, with nearly half a million Australian visitors visiting Fiji last year.

item.title: King Khan pleads guilty to breaching restraining order charges
item.description: Imran Khan, also known as 'King Khan', will be sentenced on the 14th of next month, after he pleaded guilty to the breach of court

item.title: Google data centre, Fiji Water citrus farms, Aitken Spence and BPO key investors - Kamikamica
item.description: He says one of the government's key policy reforms was the repeal of Media Industry Development Act, which he believes has significantly changed the country's dynamics by allowing people to speak more freely and enabling greater interaction and openness from the private sector. The former Deputy Prime Minister says the level of discussion and debate seen on social media today is a result of that c
item.content:encoded:
He says one of the government's key policy reforms was the repeal of Media Industry Development Act, which he believes has significantly changed the country's dynamics by allowing people to speak more freely and enabling greater interaction and openness from the private sector.

The former Deputy Prime Minister says the level of discussion and debate seen on social media today is a result of that change.

He says the mahogany industry, which currently brings in about $20 million a year, also has significant potential, saying it has remained largely untapped despite being a billion-dollar industry.

Kamikamica says the recently passed Mahogany Bill could help unlock that potential and create a major industry within the next few years.

He further says Fiji should consider establishing a sovereign wealth fund to help cushion the country against future economic shocks.

Kamikami notes that Fiji has faced challenges such as COVID-19 and the conflict in the Middle East, and says a sovereign wealth fund could provide financial security during similar crises in the future.

On the sugar industry, he says too much money is being spent on subsidies compared to the returns being generated and believes the industry needs restructuring.

He says many farmers have already started diversifying into other crops and that government support through the right policy settings is needed to help drive further change.

He has also highlighted the potential of the iTaukei economy, saying Fiji is missing out on significant opportunities.

Drawing comparisons with New Zealand's Māori economy, which contributes about $120 billion to that country's economy, he says reforms and better utilisation of resources such as mahogany could increase incomes for landowners and create new economic opportunities.

item.title: Constitution changes should benefit all Fijians - Nadi resident
item.description: A Nadi resident, Sitiveni Ratusawa told the Constitution Review Commission that any changes to Fiji's Constitution should benefit all citizens equally, while also calling for greater consideration of long-term prisoners and men's rights. Speaking during a consultation in Nadi, Ratusawa says that if the Constitution is amended, the changes should be enjoyed by everyone in Fiji and not just a select
item.content:encoded:
A Nadi resident, Sitiveni Ratusawa told the Constitution Review Commission that any changes to Fiji's Constitution should benefit all citizens equally, while also calling for greater consideration of long-term prisoners and men's rights.

Speaking during a consultation in Nadi, Ratusawa says that if the Constitution is amended, the changes should be enjoyed by everyone in Fiji and not just a select group.

To illustrate his point, he uses the example of a father telling his children not to smoke while continuing to smoke himself, saying rules and principles should apply equally to all.

Ratusawa is also calling on the Commission to consider the circumstances of people serving long-term prison sentences, saying their welfare and rights should be taken into account during the constitutional review process.

He further suggests that if laws exist to protect the rights of women, there should also be laws that specifically address the rights of men.

The submissions were made during the Constitution Review Commission consultation as members of the public continue to share their views on possible changes to the Constitution.

item.title: Teacher charged with alleged abduction of Year 10 student in Taveuni
item.description: A 39-year-old teacher has been charged with the alleged abduction of a Year 10 student in Taveuni. Police say the teacher and the student had allegedly been communicating through Messenger about plans to be together.
item.content:encoded:
A 39-year-old teacher has been charged with the alleged abduction of a Year 10 student in Taveuni.

Police say the teacher and the student had allegedly been communicating through Messenger about plans to be together.

The alleged communications were discovered by the teacher’s wife, who reported the matter to the school principal.

It is alleged that on the night of the 7th of June, the teacher was found with the student inside his vehicle near her home.

The matter was subsequently reported to Police, and an investigation was launched.

The teacher has now been formally charged with one count of abduction.

He is expected to appear in the Taveuni Magistrates Court today.

item.title: Father to be sentenced on June 30th for causing death of his 6-yr-old son
item.description: He was convicted of one count of manslaughter arising from a breach of duty.
item.content:encoded: He was convicted of one count of manslaughter arising from a breach of duty.

item.description: A 49-year-old man has been sentenced to 10 years imprisonment for one count of sexual assault and one count of rape of a family me
